briefdescription.para: Look up and encoding declaration in the meta tags.

detaileddescription.para: The returned string points into attribute content and can contain trailing garbage. It should be copied before modifying or freeing nodes.

simplesect.para: the encoding ot NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Creates or updates a meta tag with an encoding declaration.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE: This will not change the document content encoding.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, 1 if no head element was found or arguments are invalid and -1 if memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Determine if a given attribute is a boolean attribute.

detaileddescription.para: This doesn't handle HTML5.

parameterdescription.para: the name of the attribute to check

simplesect.para: false if the attribute is not boolean, true otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlBufPtr buf, xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNodePtr cur, int format)
memberdef.name: htmlBufNodeDumpFormat

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML document to an xmlBuf.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML node to an xmlBuffer.

detaileddescription.para: Always uses UTF-8.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (FILE *out, xmlDoc *doc, xmlNode *cur, const char *encoding, int format)
memberdef.name: htmlNodeDumpFileFormat

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML node to an xmlBuffer.

detaileddescription.para: If encoding is NULL, ASCII with HTML 4.0 named character entities will be used. This is inefficient compared to UTF-8 and might be changed in a future version.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: Same as htmlNodeDumpFileFormat with format set to 1 which is typically undesired.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of htmlNodeDumpFileFormat.

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML node to a memory, also returning the size of the result.

detaileddescription.para: It's up to the caller to free the memory.

Uses the encoding of the document. If the document has no encoding, ASCII with HTML 4.0 named character entities will be used. This is inefficient compared to UTF-8 and might be changed in a future version.

briefdescription.para: Same as htmlDocDumpMemoryFormat with format set to 1 which is typically undesired.

detaileddescription.para: Also see the warnings there. Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of htmlDocContentDumpFormatOutput.

briefdescription.para: Serialize the HTML document's DTD, if any.

detaileddescription.para: Ignores encoding and uses the encoding of the output buffer.

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML attribute.
Serialize an HTML node to an output buffer.

detaileddescription.para: If encoding is specified, it is used to create or update meta tags containing the character encoding.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlOutputBuffer *buf, xmlDoc *doc, xmlNode *cur, const char *encoding, int format)
memberdef.name: htmlNodeDumpFormatOutput

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML node to an output buffer.
Same as htmlNodeDumpFormatOutput with format set to 1 which is typically undesired.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of htmlNodeDumpFormatOutput.

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML document to an output buffer.
Same as htmlDocContentDumpFormatOutput with format set to 1 which is typically undesired.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of htmlDocContentDumpFormatOutput.

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML document to an open FILE.

detaileddescription.para: Uses the encoding of the document. If the document has no encoding, ASCII with HTML 4.0 named character entities will be used. This is inefficient compared to UTF-8 and might be changed in a future version.
Enables "formatting" unconditionally which is typically undesired.
Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of htmlNodeDumpFileFormat.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML document to a file.

detaileddescription.para: Same as htmlSaveFileFormat with encoding set to NULL and format set to 1 which is typically undesired.
Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of htmlSaveFileFormat.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *filename, xmlDoc *cur, const char *encoding, int format)
memberdef.name: htmlSaveFileFormat

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML document to a file using a given encoding.

detaileddescription.para: If filename is "-", stdout is used. This is potentially insecure and might be changed in a future version.
If encoding is NULL, ASCII with HTML 4.0 named character entities will be used. This is inefficient compared to UTF-8 and might be changed in a future version.
Sets or updates meta tags containing the character encoding.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: Serialize an HTML document to a file.

detaileddescription.para: Same as htmlSaveFileFormat with format set to 1 which is typically undesired. Also see the warnings there. Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of htmlSaveFileFormat.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.
